rawText:
From GLP-1 receptor agonists prescribed off-label for longevity to grey-market peptides promising superhuman recovery, a growing number of healthy individuals are bypassing conventional medicine in pursuit of biological enhancement -- a trend that reveals both the allure of self-optimization and the yawning gap between aspiration and evidence.

GLP-1 drugs such as semaglutide, originally developed for type 2 diabetes and obesity, have attracted particular enthusiasm because preliminary research -- mostly conducted in animals and cell cultures rather than rigorous human trials -- suggests they may ameliorate several hallmarks of ageing, including chronic low-level inflammation, mitochondrial decline, and the breakdown of nutrient-sensing pathways that keep cells functioning properly.

One mouse study indicated that GLP-1 receptor agonists could extend "healthspan," the portion of life spent in good health, yet it demonstrated no extension of lifespan itself -- a distinction that has not prevented thousands of people from seeking friendly doctors willing to prescribe the drugs off-label, effectively turning themselves into participants in an uncontrolled experiment with no endpoint in sight.

The peptide underground, which traces a clear lineage to the bodybuilding steroid culture of previous decades, has meanwhile exploded into a two-trillion-dollar wellness market where compounds like BPC-157 and ipamorelin are sold online as "research chemicals," complete with syringes and instructional videos, under the thinnest of legal fig leaves that they are not intended for human consumption.

What makes this folk pharmacology particularly precarious is that most grey-market peptides lack not only regulatory approval but also the kind of rigorous evidence base that would allow users to draw meaningful conclusions, as illustrated by BPC-157, for which only three small human studies exist -- the largest being a telephone survey of sixteen patients published in a third-tier journal.

The risks are far from theoretical: ipamorelin, which stimulates human growth hormone secretion, carries potential side effects ranging from heart disease and acromegaly to elevated cancer risk, while BPC-157's ability to promote angiogenesis -- the creation of new blood vessels essential for healing -- could equally nourish existing tumours, a possibility that will remain unresolved until proper clinical trials are conducted.

Even the seemingly straightforward science of exercise intensity turns out to be riddled with uncertainty, since the ubiquitous "220 minus your age" formula for estimating maximum heart rate, which has been gym orthodoxy for over fifty years, was derived from a 1971 paper whose data were compiled without stringent criteria and whose trend line was fitted by eye rather than by any proper statistical model.

Newer formulas, including the Tanaka equation published in 2001, have improved upon this crude estimate, yet a 2025 study comparing seven different methods against measured values in 230 subjects found that individual predictions were often off by as much as twenty beats per minute -- an error margin large enough to render one person's moderate workout another's dangerously vigorous exertion.

Playing a musical instrument, by contrast, represents one of the more robustly supported forms of cognitive enhancement, with studies linking regular practice to increased grey matter, improved executive function, sharper memory, and even reduced sensitivity to pain -- benefits that accrue not only to professionals but also to amateurs who engage in deliberate and consistent training throughout their lives.

The broader lesson emerging from the body-optimization craze is that the human desire to transcend biological limits consistently outpaces the science meant to guide it, and that while some interventions -- from musical training to carefully monitored exercise -- rest on a credible if incomplete evidence base, others amount to a dangerous bargain in which small, uncertain gains in performance are traded against large, unknowable risks to long-term health.

overview.title: The Body Optimization Craze: What Science Actually Says

sentences.original: From GLP-1 receptor agonists prescribed off-label for longevity to grey-market peptides promising superhuman recovery, a growing number of healthy individuals are bypassing conventional medicine in pursuit of biological enhancement -- a trend that reveals both the allure of self-optimization and the yawning gap between aspiration and evidence.
sentences.translation: 수명 연장을 위해 적응증 외 처방되는 GLP-1 수용체 작용제부터 초인적 회복을 약속하는 회색시장 펩타이드까지, 점점 더 많은 건강한 사람들이 생물학적 향상을 추구하며 기존 의학을 우회하고 있다 -- 자기 최적화의 매력과 열망과 근거 사이의 벌어진 간극을 동시에 드러내는 추세이다.

grammarElements.text: From GLP-1 receptor agonists ... to grey-market peptides ...
grammarElements.label: from A to B 범위 표현

grammarElements.text: a growing number of healthy individuals
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: are bypassing
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 동사

grammarElements.text: conventional medicine
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: a trend that reveals both ... and ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절 + both A and B

vocabulary.example: Many doctors prescribe drugs off-label when no approved treatment exists.
There is a yawning gap between what is promised and what is delivered.

expressions.usage: 목표를 향해 적극적으로 노력하는 행위를 묘사할 때 쓰는 격식 표현

patterns.pattern: From [extreme A] to [extreme B], [subject] is [verb-ing] -- a trend that reveals [insight]
patterns.explanation: 현상의 스펙트럼을 양 극단으로 보여준 뒤 그 의미를 해석하는 도입 패턴
examples: From luxury retreats to budget supplements, consumers are chasing longevity -- a trend that reveals deep anxiety about ageing.
From AI tutors to brain implants, learners are seeking shortcuts -- a trend that reveals both hope and impatience.

drillQuestions.question: A growing number of healthy individuals are ______ conventional medicine in pursuit of biological enhancement.

sentences.notes: from A to B 구조가 현상의 넓은 스펙트럼을 설정하고, 대시 뒤의 동격이 기사 전체의 핵심 긴장 -- 열망 대 근거 -- 를 미리 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.ko: 적응증 외 처방되는 GLP-1 수용체 작용제

koreanAlignment.en: the yawning gap between aspiration and evidence

sentences.original: GLP-1 drugs such as semaglutide, originally developed for type 2 diabetes and obesity, have attracted particular enthusiasm because preliminary research -- mostly conducted in animals and cell cultures rather than rigorous human trials -- suggests they may ameliorate several hallmarks of ageing, including chronic low-level inflammation, mitochondrial decline, and the breakdown of nutrient-sensing pathways that keep cells functioning properly.
sentences.translation: 원래 2형 당뇨병과 비만을 위해 개발된 세마글루타이드 같은 GLP-1 약물은 특별한 열광을 끌어모았다. 예비 연구 -- 엄격한 인체 시험이 아닌 주로 동물과 세포 배양에서 수행된 -- 가 이 약물이 만성 저수준 염증, 미토콘드리아 쇠퇴, 세포가 제대로 기능하게 유지하는 영양소 감지 경로의 붕괴를 포함한 노화의 여러 특징을 개선할 수 있음을 시사하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: GLP-1 drugs such as semaglutide
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: originally developed for type 2 diabetes and obesity
grammarElements.label: 축약된 과거분사 수식어

grammarElements.text: have attracted
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: because preliminary research ... suggests they may ameliorate ...
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 + 삽입구

vocabulary.example: The treatment may ameliorate symptoms but cannot cure the underlying disease.

expressions.usage: 노화 연구에서 노화를 규정하는 핵심 생물학적 과정들을 통칭하는 학술 표현

patterns.pattern: [Drug], originally developed for [purpose A], has attracted enthusiasm because [research] suggests it may [benefit B]
patterns.explanation: 약물의 원래 용도와 새로운 기대 사이의 간극을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Metformin, originally developed for diabetes, has attracted interest because studies suggest it may slow ageing.
Ketamine, originally used as an anaesthetic, has drawn attention because trials suggest it may treat depression.

drillQuestions.question: Preliminary research suggests GLP-1 drugs may ______ several hallmarks of ageing.

sentences.notes: 삽입 대시 안의 mostly conducted in animals가 연구의 한계를 조용히 밝히며, 열광과 근거 사이의 괴리를 독자에게 알린다.

sentences.original: One mouse study indicated that GLP-1 receptor agonists could extend "healthspan," the portion of life spent in good health, yet it demonstrated no extension of lifespan itself -- a distinction that has not prevented thousands of people from seeking friendly doctors willing to prescribe the drugs off-label, effectively turning themselves into participants in an uncontrolled experiment with no endpoint in sight.
sentences.translation: 한 쥐 실험은 GLP-1 수용체 작용제가 건강 수명 -- 건강한 상태로 보내는 삶의 비율 -- 을 연장할 수 있음을 보여주었지만, 수명 자체의 연장은 입증하지 못했다 -- 이 구분이 수천 명의 사람들이 적응증 외 처방에 응해줄 호의적인 의사를 찾는 것을 막지는 못했고, 그들은 사실상 종료 시점이 보이지 않는 비통제 실험의 참가자로 스스로를 전환시키고 있다.

grammarElements.text: One mouse study
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: indicated that ... could extend ..., yet it demonstrated no extension
grammarElements.label: 과거시제 동사 + yet 대조

grammarElements.text: a distinction that has not prevented ... from ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절 + prevent A from B

grammarElements.text: effectively turning themselves into participants
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Researchers are increasingly focused on extending healthspan, not just lifespan.

expressions.usage: 언제 끝날지 알 수 없는 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Study] indicated [positive finding], yet it demonstrated no [expected outcome] -- a distinction that has not prevented [people] from [action]
patterns.explanation: 연구 결과의 제한적 성과와 그럼에도 불구한 대중의 행동을 대조하는 패턴
examples: The trial indicated modest improvement in mood, yet it demonstrated no reduction in relapse rates -- a nuance that has not prevented clinics from marketing the treatment as a cure.
The data showed a correlation with longevity, yet it demonstrated no causal link -- a subtlety that has not stopped supplement makers from making bold claims.

drillQuestions.question: This distinction has not ______ thousands of people from seeking off-label prescriptions.

sentences.notes: healthspan과 lifespan의 구분이 이 문장의 핵심이다. effectively라는 부사가 사람들이 의도치 않게 실험 대상이 되고 있다는 아이러니를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the portion of life spent in good health
an uncontrolled experiment with no endpoint in sight
koreanAlignment.ko: 종료 시점이 보이지 않는 비통제 실험

sentences.original: The peptide underground, which traces a clear lineage to the bodybuilding steroid culture of previous decades, has meanwhile exploded into a two-trillion-dollar wellness market where compounds like BPC-157 and ipamorelin are sold online as "research chemicals," complete with syringes and instructional videos, under the thinnest of legal fig leaves that they are not intended for human consumption.
sentences.translation: 이전 수십 년간의 보디빌딩 스테로이드 문화에 뚜렷한 계보를 둔 펩타이드 지하 시장은, 그 사이 2조 달러 규모의 웰니스 시장으로 폭발적으로 성장했다. BPC-157과 이파모렐린 같은 화합물이 주사기와 사용 안내 영상이 딸린 '연구용 화학물질'로 온라인에서 판매되며, '인체 섭취용이 아님'이라는 가장 얇은 법적 구실 아래 거래된다.

grammarElements.text: The peptide underground
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which traces a clear lineage to ...
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

grammarElements.text: has meanwhile exploded into
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: a two-trillion-dollar wellness market where ...
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + where 관계절

grammarElements.text: under the thinnest of legal fig leaves that ...
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 동격 that절

vocabulary.example: The company traces its lineage to a small workshop founded in 1920.

expressions.usage: 실질적 효력은 없지만 형식적으로 문제를 가리기 위한 장치를 비유적으로 묘사

patterns.pattern: [Underground market], which traces a lineage to [origin], has exploded into [massive market] where [products] are sold under [thin legal cover]
patterns.explanation: 지하 시장이 주류로 성장한 과정과 그 법적 회색지대를 한 문장으로 설명하는 패턴
examples: The crypto underground, which traces a lineage to cypherpunk forums, has exploded into a trillion-dollar market where tokens are sold under minimal regulatory oversight.
The nootropics underground, which traces a lineage to Silicon Valley biohacking, has grown into a billion-dollar industry where supplements are marketed under loose health claims.

drillQuestions.question: These compounds are sold under the thinnest of legal fig ______ that they are not intended for human consumption.

sentences.notes: fig leaf라는 성경적 비유가 법적 구실의 허약함을 직관적으로 전달한다. complete with이 주사기와 영상까지 갖춘 판매 실태의 노골적 성격을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: complete with syringes and instructional videos
the thinnest of legal fig leaves

pronunciationNotes: lineage [ˈlɪniɪdʒ] -- '리니이지'로 첫 음절 강세, 세 음절

sentences.original: What makes this folk pharmacology particularly precarious is that most grey-market peptides lack not only regulatory approval but also the kind of rigorous evidence base that would allow users to draw meaningful conclusions, as illustrated by BPC-157, for which only three small human studies exist -- the largest being a telephone survey of sixteen patients published in a third-tier journal.
sentences.translation: 이 민간 약리학을 특히 위태롭게 만드는 것은 대부분의 회색시장 펩타이드에 규제 승인뿐 아니라 사용자가 의미 있는 결론을 내릴 수 있게 해줄 엄격한 근거 기반도 부재하다는 점이다. BPC-157이 이를 잘 보여준다 -- 인체 연구가 소규모로 세 건만 존재하며, 가장 큰 것이 3류 저널에 실린 16명 환자 전화 설문조사이다.

grammarElements.text: What makes this folk pharmacology particularly precarious
grammarElements.label: What절 주어

grammarElements.text: is that
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 명사절 접속사

grammarElements.text: most grey-market peptides lack not only ... but also ...
grammarElements.label: 상관접속사 구조

grammarElements.text: as illustrated by BPC-157, for which ...
grammarElements.label: 예시 분사구문 + 전치사 관계절

vocabulary.example: The company's financial position remains precarious despite recent cost cuts.

expressions.usage: 과학적 근거 없이 대중이 스스로 약물을 선택하고 투여하는 현상을 비판적으로 묘사

patterns.pattern: What makes [phenomenon] particularly [adjective] is that [subject] lacks not only [A] but also [B]
patterns.explanation: 문제의 핵심이 이중적 결핍에 있음을 강조하는 What절 구조
examples: What makes this investment particularly risky is that the company lacks not only revenue but also a clear business model.
What makes the diagnosis particularly difficult is that the condition lacks not only distinctive symptoms but also reliable biomarkers.

drillQuestions.question: What makes this folk pharmacology particularly ______ is the lack of rigorous evidence.

sentences.notes: the largest being a telephone survey of sixteen patients라는 구체적 디테일이 근거 기반의 빈약함을 극적으로 보여준다. 숫자의 작음 자체가 논거가 된다.

koreanAlignment.en: a telephone survey of sixteen patients

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: reductio ad absurdum (극단적 예시를 통한 논파)

sentences.original: The risks are far from theoretical: ipamorelin, which stimulates human growth hormone secretion, carries potential side effects ranging from heart disease and acromegaly to elevated cancer risk, while BPC-157's ability to promote angiogenesis -- the creation of new blood vessels essential for healing -- could equally nourish existing tumours, a possibility that will remain unresolved until proper clinical trials are conducted.
sentences.translation: 위험은 이론적인 것과는 거리가 멀다. 인체 성장호르몬 분비를 자극하는 이파모렐린은 심장병과 말단비대증에서 암 위험 증가에 이르는 잠재적 부작용을 수반하며, 혈관 신생 -- 치유에 필수적인 새 혈관의 생성 -- 을 촉진하는 BPC-157의 능력은 기존 종양에도 똑같이 영양을 공급할 수 있다. 이 가능성은 적절한 임상 시험이 수행될 때까지 해소되지 않을 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The risks
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: are far from theoretical
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: ipamorelin ... carries potential side effects ranging from ... to ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 예시절

grammarElements.text: while BPC-157's ability ... could equally nourish ...
grammarElements.label: while 대조절

vocabulary.example: Anti-angiogenesis drugs are used to starve tumours of their blood supply.

expressions.meaning: 이론적인 것과는 거리가 먼, 매우 현실적인
expressions.usage: 위험이 가설이 아닌 실제로 존재함을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The risks are far from theoretical: [specific compound] carries [side effects], while [another compound]'s [benefit] could equally [cause harm]
patterns.explanation: 위험이 추상적이 아닌 구체적임을 두 가지 사례로 입증하는 패턴
examples: The dangers are far from hypothetical: one supplement has been linked to liver damage, while another's anti-inflammatory properties could mask symptoms of serious illness.
The concerns are far from abstract: algorithm A has produced biased outcomes, while algorithm B's efficiency could equally amplify existing inequalities.

drillQuestions.question: BPC-157's ability to promote ______ could equally nourish existing tumours.

sentences.notes: 치유를 위한 혈관 신생이 종양에도 영양을 공급할 수 있다는 양날의 검 논리가 핵심. could equally라는 표현이 같은 메커니즘의 이중성을 간결하게 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: ranging from heart disease to elevated cancer risk
the creation of new blood vessels

koreanAlignment.ko: 기존 종양에도 똑같이 영양을 공급할 수 있다

sentences.original: Even the seemingly straightforward science of exercise intensity turns out to be riddled with uncertainty, since the ubiquitous "220 minus your age" formula for estimating maximum heart rate, which has been gym orthodoxy for over fifty years, was derived from a 1971 paper whose data were compiled without stringent criteria and whose trend line was fitted by eye rather than by any proper statistical model.
sentences.translation: 운동 강도라는 겉보기에 간단한 과학조차 불확실성으로 가득 차 있는 것으로 밝혀진다. 50년 넘게 헬스장의 정설이었던 최대 심박수 추정 공식 '220에서 나이를 빼라'가, 엄격한 기준 없이 자료가 수집되었고 추세선이 적절한 통계 모형이 아닌 눈대중으로 맞춰진 1971년 논문에서 유래했기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Even the seemingly straightforward science of exercise intensity
grammarElements.label: 주어 (Even 강조)

grammarElements.text: turns out to be riddled with
grammarElements.label: turn out to be 구문

grammarElements.text: uncertainty
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: since the ubiquitous ... formula ... was derived from a 1971 paper whose ...
grammarElements.label: since 이유절 + 이중 whose 관계절

vocabulary.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern classrooms.
The new regulations imposed stringent criteria on clinical trials.

expressions.usage: 문제나 결함이 광범위하게 퍼져 있음을 부정적으로 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Even [seemingly simple subject] turns out to be riddled with [problem], since [foundational source] was derived from [flawed method]
patterns.explanation: 당연하게 받아들여진 것도 실은 근거가 부실했음을 폭로하는 패턴
examples: Even the standard dietary guidelines turn out to be riddled with gaps, since the original studies were based on small, unrepresentative samples.
Even basic calorie counting turns out to be riddled with inaccuracies, since food labels rely on averages rather than individual measurements.

drillQuestions.question: The formula was derived from a 1971 paper whose trend line was fitted by ______ rather than by a statistical model.

sentences.notes: fitted by eye라는 디테일이 과학적 정밀성의 부재를 유머러스하면서도 충격적으로 드러낸다. whose가 두 번 쓰이며 논문의 이중적 결함을 나열한다.

koreanAlignment.en: gym orthodoxy for over fifty years

sentences.original: Newer formulas, including the Tanaka equation published in 2001, have improved upon this crude estimate, yet a 2025 study comparing seven different methods against measured values in 230 subjects found that individual predictions were often off by as much as twenty beats per minute -- an error margin large enough to render one person's moderate workout another's dangerously vigorous exertion.
sentences.translation: 2001년에 발표된 다나카 공식을 포함한 새로운 공식들이 이 조잡한 추정을 개선했지만, 230명의 피험자에서 측정값 대비 일곱 가지 방법을 비교한 2025년 연구는 개인별 예측이 분당 20회까지 벗어나는 경우가 많다는 것을 발견했다 -- 한 사람의 적당한 운동을 다른 사람의 위험할 정도로 격렬한 운동으로 만들 수 있을 만큼 큰 오차 범위이다.

grammarElements.text: Newer formulas, including the Tanaka equation
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 삽입 포함

grammarElements.text: have improved upon
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: this crude estimate
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: yet a 2025 study ... found that ...
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절

grammarElements.text: an error margin large enough to render ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + enough to 구문

vocabulary.example: A single miscalculation can render an entire model useless.

expressions.usage: 측정이나 예측의 오차 범위를 구체적 수치와 함께 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Improved method] has improved upon [old method], yet [study] found that [predictions] were often off by [margin] -- an error large enough to [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 개선되었음에도 여전히 중요한 한계가 남아 있음을 수치와 결과로 보여주는 패턴
examples: Newer tests have improved upon the original screening, yet a review found that false positives still occurred in one in five cases -- a rate high enough to cause unnecessary anxiety.
Updated models have improved upon early estimates, yet a study found that projections were still off by decades -- an error large enough to undermine policy planning.

drillQuestions.question: Individual predictions were often off by as much as twenty ______ per minute.

sentences.notes: render A B 구문이 오차의 실질적 위험을 한 단어로 압축한다. 분당 20회의 오차가 구체적으로 얼마나 위험한지 대비를 통해 체감시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: off by as much as twenty beats per minute
an error margin large enough to
one person's moderate workout another's dangerously vigorous exertion
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 사람의 적당한 운동을 다른 사람의 위험한 격렬 운동으로

pronunciationNotes: render [ˈrendər] -- '렌더'로 첫 음절 강세, 두 음절

sentences.original: Playing a musical instrument, by contrast, represents one of the more robustly supported forms of cognitive enhancement, with studies linking regular practice to increased grey matter, improved executive function, sharper memory, and even reduced sensitivity to pain -- benefits that accrue not only to professionals but also to amateurs who engage in deliberate and consistent training throughout their lives.
sentences.translation: 이와 대조적으로, 악기 연주는 인지 향상의 가장 탄탄하게 뒷받침되는 형태 중 하나이다. 정기적 연습이 회백질 증가, 실행 기능 향상, 기억력 강화, 심지어 통증 민감도 감소와 연결된다는 연구들이 있다 -- 전문가뿐 아니라 평생에 걸쳐 의도적이고 꾸준한 훈련에 참여하는 아마추어에게도 축적되는 혜택이다.

grammarElements.text: Playing a musical instrument
grammarElements.label: 동명사 주어

grammarElements.text: by contrast
grammarElements.label: 대조 삽입어

grammarElements.text: represents
grammarElements.label: 현재시제 동사

grammarElements.text: one of the more robustly supported forms of cognitive enhancement
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: with studies linking regular practice to ...
grammarElements.label: with + 분사구문

vocabulary.example: Health benefits accrue over years of consistent practice, not from a single session.

expressions.usage: 앞선 부정적 내용과 대비되는 긍정적 사례를 도입할 때 쓰는 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: [Activity], by contrast, represents one of the more robustly supported forms of [benefit], with studies linking [practice] to [outcome 1], [outcome 2], and [outcome 3]
patterns.explanation: 앞선 부정적 사례들과 대비하여 근거가 탄탄한 대안을 제시하는 패턴
examples: Regular aerobic exercise, by contrast, represents one of the more solidly supported forms of heart protection, with studies linking daily walking to lower blood pressure, improved cholesterol, and reduced stroke risk.

Reading aloud, by contrast, represents one of the more reliably validated forms of language development, with studies linking daily practice to expanded vocabulary, better comprehension, and stronger fluency.

drillQuestions.question: Benefits ______ not only to professionals but also to amateurs who engage in consistent training.

sentences.notes: 기사의 비판적 논조 속에서 악기 연주를 긍정적 예외로 제시하는 전환점 역할을 한다. robustly supported라는 표현이 앞선 문단들의 '근거 부재'와 명확히 대조된다.

koreanAlignment.en: robustly supported forms of cognitive enhancement

sentences.original: The broader lesson emerging from the body-optimization craze is that the human desire to transcend biological limits consistently outpaces the science meant to guide it, and that while some interventions -- from musical training to carefully monitored exercise -- rest on a credible if incomplete evidence base, others amount to a dangerous bargain in which small, uncertain gains in performance are traded against large, unknowable risks to long-term health.
sentences.translation: 신체 최적화 열풍에서 떠오르는 더 넓은 교훈은, 생물학적 한계를 초월하려는 인간의 욕망이 그것을 이끌어야 할 과학을 지속적으로 앞지른다는 것이며, 악기 훈련에서 신중하게 모니터링된 운동에 이르는 일부 개입은 불완전하더라도 신뢰할 만한 근거 기반 위에 서 있는 반면, 다른 개입들은 작고 불확실한 수행 능력 향상을 크고 알 수 없는 장기 건강 위험과 맞바꾸는 위험한 거래에 해당한다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The broader lesson emerging from the body-optimization craze
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 현재분사 수식

grammarElements.text: is that ... and that ...
grammarElements.label: be동사 + 이중 that절

grammarElements.text: the human desire to transcend biological limits consistently outpaces the science
grammarElements.label: 첫 번째 that절

grammarElements.text: while some interventions ... rest on ..., others amount to ...
grammarElements.label: while 대조 + some ... others 구조

vocabulary.example: The desire to transcend human limitations has driven both scientific progress and reckless experimentation.
What looks like a bargain may turn out to carry hidden costs.

expressions.usage: 어떤 행위나 상황의 본질을 평가적으로 규정할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: The broader lesson is that [desire] consistently outpaces [science], and that while some [interventions] rest on [evidence], others amount to [danger]
patterns.explanation: 기사의 결론으로서 핵심 긴장을 요약하고 두 부류의 개입을 구분하는 패턴
examples: The broader lesson is that investor appetite consistently outpaces regulatory capacity, and that while some innovations rest on sound principles, others amount to speculation dressed as progress.
The broader lesson is that political ambition consistently outpaces institutional readiness, and that while some reforms rest on evidence, others amount to experiments on the public.

drillQuestions.question: The human desire to ______ biological limits consistently outpaces the science meant to guide it.

sentences.notes: 기사의 최종 결론문. some ... others 대조가 '근거 있는 개입'과 '근거 없는 개입'을 명확히 나누며, dangerous bargain이라는 비유가 위험의 비대칭성을 압축적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the human desire to transcend biological limits
a credible if incomplete evidence base
small, uncertain gains ... traded against large, unknowable risks
koreanAlignment.ko: 작고 불확실한 이득을 크고 알 수 없는 위험과 맞바꾸다
